description: Shared TS<->Python parity fixtures for the hent-ai anti-fixation pure core. Each scenario is evaluated by openclaw/watcher-core.ts (evaluateFixtureResult) and hermes/watcher_core.py and MUST produce identical fixated/patterns. Defaults: windowN=8, persistenceK=3, simThreshold=0.6, persistenceSimFloor=0.4.

fixtures.name: self_repetition_positive
fixtures.description: Agent restates near-identical content across three turns with no user correction.

rawMessages.text: I think we should ship the release today for sure.
I think we should ship the release today for sure.
I think we should ship the release today for sure.

fixtures.name: legit_elaboration_negative
fixtures.description: Agent advances the deployment topic with genuinely new substance each turn (low similarity, shifting frame).

rawMessages.text: Let me outline the deployment steps for the staging environment.
First we provision the database and run schema migrations carefully.
Then configure load balancers and verify health checks end to end.

fixtures.name: user_driven_continuation_negative
fixtures.description: User keeps a topic alive with questions; agent replies vary substantially.

rawMessages.text: what is the weather like today?
It is sunny and warm around 24 degrees this afternoon.
Tomorrow brings light rain in the morning then clearing skies.

fixtures.name: similarity_boundary_positive
fixtures.description: Two agent turns whose token-set Jaccard is exactly 0.6 (3 shared of 5 union); fires at the epsilon-compared threshold.

fixtures.name: correction_driven_positive
fixtures.description: Agent repeats the same frame after the user explicitly redirects; isolates the secondary correction signal (low textual similarity, run<K so no stale signal).

rawMessages.text: should deploy staging server right away before lunch
stop, focus on the billing bug instead
should deploy staging server because priority today honestly
